modules.comments:
Linear algebra for graphics programming.

This module re-exports the core functionality from the linear package.
It provides fixed-size vectors (V2, V3, V4), quaternions for 3D rotations,
and matrix operations commonly used in graphics and game development.

## Quick Start

```purescript
import Linear

-- 2D vector operations
v1 = V2 3.0 4.0
v2 = V2 1.0 0.0
sum = v1 ^+^ v2         -- V2 4.0 4.0
length = norm v1        -- 5.0
unit = signorm v1       -- V2 0.6 0.8

-- 3D cross product
x = V3 1.0 0.0 0.0
y = V3 0.0 1.0 0.0
z = cross x y           -- V3 0.0 0.0 1.0

-- Quaternion rotation
q = axisAngle (V3 0.0 1.0 0.0) (pi / 2.0)  -- 90° around Y
rotated = rotate q (V3 1.0 0.0 0.0)         -- ≈ V3 0.0 0.0 (-1.0)
```
modules.name: Linear

children.comments: Points form an affine space over their underlying vectors.
children.title: <anonymous>

declarations.comments:
A point in an affine space.

The `Point` newtype distinguishes points from vectors at the type level.
This prevents nonsensical operations like adding two points together.

```purescript
origin = P (V2 0.0 0.0)
position = P (V2 3.0 4.0)
```
declarations.title: Point

children.comments: The vector from the first point to the second.
children.title: diff

children.comments: Add a vector to a point.
children.title: moveBy

children.comments: Subtract a vector from a point.
children.title: moveByNeg

children.comments: Points form an affine space over their underlying vectors.
children.title: <anonymous>

declarations.comments:
An affine space is a set of points with associated difference vectors.

Laws:
- `p .+^ (q .-. p) = q`
- `(p .+^ u) .+^ v = p .+^ (u ^+^ v)`
- `p .-. p = zero`
declarations.title: Affine

declarations.comments: Unwrap a point to get the underlying vector.
declarations.title: unP

declarations.comments:
The squared distance between two points.

More efficient than `distanceA` when you only need to compare distances.

```purescript
qdA (P (V2 0.0 0.0)) (P (V2 3.0 4.0)) = 25.0
```
declarations.title: qdA

declarations.comments:
The origin point (all coordinates zero).

```purescript
origin :: Point V3 Number
origin = P (V3 0.0 0.0 0.0)
```
declarations.title: origin

declarations.comments:
Linear interpolation between two points.

```purescript
lerpP 0.0 p1 p2 = p1
lerpP 1.0 p1 p2 = p2
lerpP 0.5 p1 p2 -- midpoint
```
declarations.title: lerpP

declarations.comments:
The distance between two points.

```purescript
distanceA (P (V2 0.0 0.0)) (P (V2 3.0 4.0)) = 5.0
```
declarations.title: distanceA

children.comments: For Maybe, Nothing is considered near zero, and Just delegates.
children.title: <anonymous>

declarations.comments:
A typeclass for types that support approximate zero testing.

The `nearZero` function provides a "fairly subjective test to see
if a quantity is near zero" (as Ed Kmett puts it).
declarations.title: Epsilon

declarations.comments:
Build a 4x4 transformation matrix from a quaternion rotation and translation.

The resulting matrix applies rotation first, then translation.

```purescript
mkTransformation identity (V3 1.0 2.0 3.0) -- pure translation
mkTransformation rotation (V3 0.0 0.0 0.0) -- pure rotation
```
declarations.title: mkTransformation

declarations.title: inv44

Convert a unit quaternion to a 3x3 rotation matrix.

```purescript
fromQuaternion (Quaternion 1.0 (V3 0.0 0.0 0.0)) = identity33
```

children.comments:
The inner (dot) product of two vectors.

```purescript
dot (V2 1.0 2.0) (V2 3.0 4.0) = 11.0  -- 1*3 + 2*4
```
children.title: dot

children.comments:
The squared norm of a vector (quadrance from rational trigonometry).

This is more efficient than `norm` when you only need to compare magnitudes.

```purescript
quadrance (V2 3.0 4.0) = 25.0  -- 3² + 4²
```
children.title: quadrance

children.comments:
The squared distance between two vectors.

```purescript
qd (V2 0.0 0.0) (V2 3.0 4.0) = 25.0
```
children.title: qd

children.comments:
The Euclidean distance between two vectors.

```purescript
distance (V2 0.0 0.0) (V2 3.0 4.0) = 5.0
```
children.title: distance

children.comments:
The Euclidean norm (magnitude) of a vector.

```purescript
norm (V2 3.0 4.0) = 5.0
```
children.title: norm

children.comments:
Convert a non-zero vector to a unit vector (signorm = "sign of norm").

Returns the zero vector when given the zero vector.

```purescript
signorm (V2 3.0 4.0) = V2 0.6 0.8
signorm (V2 0.0 0.0) = V2 0.0 0.0
```
children.title: signorm

declarations.comments:
A metric space with an inner product.

Laws:
- `dot v v >= 0` (positive semi-definite)
- `dot v v = 0` implies `v = zero`
- `dot u v = dot v u` (symmetry)
- `dot (a *^ u) v = a * dot u v` (linearity)
declarations.title: Metric

declarations.comments:
Project the first vector onto the second.

```purescript
project (V2 1.0 0.0) (V2 1.0 1.0) = V2 0.5 0.5
```
declarations.title: project

declarations.comments:
Normalize a vector to unit length.

Unlike `signorm`, this function handles the zero vector by returning
zero rather than NaN.

```purescript
normalize (V2 3.0 4.0) = V2 0.6 0.8
normalize (V2 0.0 0.0) = V2 0.0 0.0
```
declarations.title: normalize

declarations.comments:
A quaternion representing a rotation in 3D space.

A quaternion q = w + xi + yj + zk is stored as `Quaternion w (V3 x y z)`.
Unit quaternions (where |q| = 1) represent rotations.

```purescript
identity = Quaternion 1.0 (V3 0.0 0.0 0.0)
```
declarations.title: Quaternion

declarations.comments:
Spherical linear interpolation between two quaternions.

This produces smooth rotation interpolation along the shortest path.

```purescript
slerp 0.0 q1 q2 = q1
slerp 1.0 q1 q2 = q2
slerp 0.5 q1 q2 -- halfway rotation
```
declarations.title: slerp

declarations.comments:
Rotate a 3D vector by a quaternion.

The quaternion should be a unit quaternion for proper rotation.

```purescript
let q = axisAngle (V3 0.0 1.0 0.0) (pi / 2.0)  -- 90° around Y
rotate q (V3 1.0 0.0 0.0) ≈ V3 0.0 0.0 (-1.0)
```
declarations.title: rotate

declarations.comments:
Quaternion multiplication (Hamilton product).

This is NOT commutative: `qmul q1 q2 /= qmul q2 q1` in general.
Composing rotations: to apply rotation q1 then q2, use `qmul q2 q1`.

```purescript
qmul identity q = q
qmul q identity = q
```
declarations.title: qmul

declarations.comments:
The multiplicative inverse of a quaternion.

For unit quaternions, this equals the conjugate.

```purescript
qmul q (inverse q) ≈ identity
```
declarations.title: inverse

declarations.comments:
Construct a rotation from Euler angles (in radians).

Order is ZYX (yaw, pitch, roll).

```purescript
fromEuler 0.0 0.0 0.0 = identity
```
declarations.title: fromEuler

declarations.comments:
The conjugate of a quaternion.

For unit quaternions, the conjugate equals the inverse and
represents the opposite rotation.

```purescript
conjugate (Quaternion w (V3 x y z)) = Quaternion w (V3 (-x) (-y) (-z))
```
declarations.title: conjugate

declarations.comments:
Construct a rotation quaternion from an axis and angle.

The axis should be a unit vector. The angle is in radians.

```purescript
-- 90 degree rotation around the Y axis
axisAngle (V3 0.0 1.0 0.0) (pi / 2.0)
```
declarations.title: axisAngle

declarations.comments:
A 2-dimensional vector.

```purescript
origin = V2 0.0 0.0
direction = V2 1.0 0.0
```
declarations.title: V2

declarations.comments:
Extract the angle (in radians) from a vector, measured from the positive x-axis.

```purescript
unangle (V2 1.0 0.0) = 0.0
unangle (V2 0.0 1.0) = pi / 2.0
```
declarations.title: unangle

declarations.comments:
The counter-clockwise perpendicular vector.

```purescript
perp (V2 1.0 0.0) = V2 0.0 1.0
perp (V2 0.0 1.0) = V2 (-1.0) 0.0
```
declarations.title: perp

declarations.comments:
The z-component of the cross product of two 2D vectors.

This is useful for determining the orientation of two vectors:
- Positive: v2 is counter-clockwise from v1
- Negative: v2 is clockwise from v1
- Zero: vectors are parallel

```purescript
crossZ (V2 1.0 0.0) (V2 0.0 1.0) = 1.0  -- counter-clockwise
crossZ (V2 0.0 1.0) (V2 1.0 0.0) = -1.0 -- clockwise
```
declarations.title: crossZ

declarations.comments:
Construct a unit vector at the given angle (in radians) from the positive x-axis.

```purescript
angle 0.0 = V2 1.0 0.0
angle (pi / 2.0) ≈ V2 0.0 1.0
```
declarations.title: angle

declarations.comments:
A 3-dimensional vector.

```purescript
origin = V3 0.0 0.0 0.0
up = V3 0.0 1.0 0.0
```
declarations.title: V3

declarations.comments:
The scalar triple product of three vectors.

This equals the signed volume of the parallelepiped formed by the vectors.

```purescript
triple (V3 1.0 0.0 0.0) (V3 0.0 1.0 0.0) (V3 0.0 0.0 1.0) = 1.0
```
declarations.title: triple

declarations.comments:
The cross product of two 3D vectors.

The result is perpendicular to both input vectors, with magnitude
equal to the area of the parallelogram they span.

```purescript
cross (V3 1.0 0.0 0.0) (V3 0.0 1.0 0.0) = V3 0.0 0.0 1.0
```
declarations.title: cross

declarations.comments:
A 4-dimensional vector.

Often used for homogeneous coordinates where (x, y, z, w) represents
the 3D point (x/w, y/w, z/w).

```purescript
origin = V4 0.0 0.0 0.0 1.0
direction = V4 1.0 0.0 0.0 0.0
```
declarations.title: V4

declarations.comments:
Convert a 3D vector to homogeneous coordinates with w=0.

This represents a direction (not a point) in homogeneous coordinates.

```purescript
vector (V3 1.0 0.0 0.0) = V4 1.0 0.0 0.0 0.0
```
declarations.title: vector

declarations.comments:
Convert a 3D point to homogeneous coordinates with w=1.

This represents a point (not a direction) in homogeneous coordinates.

```purescript
point (V3 1.0 2.0 3.0) = V4 1.0 2.0 3.0 1.0
```
declarations.title: point

declarations.comments:
Convert homogeneous coordinates back to 3D by dividing by w.

```purescript
normalizePoint (V4 2.0 4.0 6.0 2.0) = V3 1.0 2.0 3.0
```
declarations.title: normalizePoint

children.comments: The zero vector
children.title: zero

children.comments: Vector addition
children.title: add

children.comments: Vector subtraction
children.title: sub

children.comments:
Linear interpolation: `lerp t a b = a + t * (b - a)`

- `lerp 0.0 a b = a`
- `lerp 1.0 a b = b`
children.title: lerp

children.comments: Apply a function to non-zero values (union semantics)
children.title: liftU2

children.comments: Apply a function component-wise (intersection semantics)
children.title: liftI2

declarations.comments:
A vector space with addition and scalar multiplication.

Laws:
- `add zero v = v` (left identity)
- `add v zero = v` (right identity)
- `add (add u v) w = add u (add v w)` (associativity)
- `add u v = add v u` (commutativity)
- `sub v v = zero`
declarations.title: Additive

declarations.comments: Sum a collection of vectors
declarations.title: sumV

declarations.comments:
Negate a vector

```purescript
negated (V2 1.0 (-2.0)) = V2 (-1.0) 2.0
```
declarations.title: negated

modules.comments:
Affine spaces and points.

This module provides the `Affine` typeclass for affine spaces,
where points and vectors are distinguished at the type level.
Points can be subtracted to yield vectors, and vectors can be
added to points, but points cannot be added to each other.

children.comments: Points form an affine space over their underlying vectors.
children.title: <anonymous>

declarations.comments:
A point in an affine space.

The `Point` newtype distinguishes points from vectors at the type level.
This prevents nonsensical operations like adding two points together.

```purescript
origin = P (V2 0.0 0.0)
position = P (V2 3.0 4.0)
```
declarations.title: Point

declarations.comments: Unwrap a point to get the underlying vector.
declarations.title: unP

children.comments: The vector from the first point to the second.

children.comments: Add a vector to a point.

children.comments: Points form an affine space over their underlying vectors.
children.title: <anonymous>

declarations.comments:
An affine space is a set of points with associated difference vectors.

Laws:
- `p .+^ (q .-. p) = q`
- `(p .+^ u) .+^ v = p .+^ (u ^+^ v)`
- `p .-. p = zero`
declarations.title: Affine

declarations.comments:
The origin point (all coordinates zero).

```purescript
origin :: Point V3 Number
origin = P (V3 0.0 0.0 0.0)
```
declarations.title: origin

declarations.comments:
The distance between two points.

```purescript
distanceA (P (V2 0.0 0.0)) (P (V2 3.0 4.0)) = 5.0
```
declarations.title: distanceA

declarations.comments:
The squared distance between two points.

More efficient than `distanceA` when you only need to compare distances.

```purescript
qdA (P (V2 0.0 0.0)) (P (V2 3.0 4.0)) = 25.0
```
declarations.title: qdA

declarations.comments:
Linear interpolation between two points.

```purescript
lerpP 0.0 p1 p2 = p1
lerpP 1.0 p1 p2 = p2
lerpP 0.5 p1 p2 -- midpoint
```
declarations.title: lerpP

modules.name: Linear.Affine

modules.comments:
Approximate equality testing for floating-point values.

This module provides the `Epsilon` typeclass for testing whether
values are "near zero", useful for numerical algorithms that need
to handle floating-point imprecision.

children.comments: For Maybe, Nothing is considered near zero, and Just delegates.
children.title: <anonymous>

declarations.comments:
A typeclass for types that support approximate zero testing.

The `nearZero` function provides a "fairly subjective test to see
if a quantity is near zero" (as Ed Kmett puts it).
declarations.title: Epsilon

modules.name: Linear.Epsilon

modules.comments:
Matrix types and operations.

Matrices are represented as vectors of row vectors, following Haskell's linear.
This module provides type aliases and operations for common matrix sizes.

declarations.comments: 2x2 matrix (2 rows, 2 columns)
declarations.title: M22

declarations.comments: 2x3 matrix
declarations.title: M23

declarations.comments: 2x4 matrix
declarations.title: M24

declarations.comments: 3x2 matrix
declarations.title: M32

declarations.comments: 3x3 matrix
declarations.title: M33

declarations.comments: 3x4 matrix
declarations.title: M34

declarations.comments: 4x2 matrix
declarations.title: M42

declarations.comments: 4x3 matrix
declarations.title: M43

declarations.comments: 4x4 matrix (commonly used for 3D transformations)
declarations.title: M44

declarations.comments: 2x2 identity matrix.
declarations.title: identity22

declarations.comments: 3x3 identity matrix.
declarations.title: identity33

declarations.comments: 4x4 identity matrix.
declarations.title: identity44

declarations.comments:
Convert a unit quaternion to a 3x3 rotation matrix.

```purescript
fromQuaternion (Quaternion 1.0 (V3 0.0 0.0 0.0)) = identity33
```
declarations.title: fromQuaternion

declarations.comments:
Build a 4x4 transformation matrix from a quaternion rotation and translation.

The resulting matrix applies rotation first, then translation.

```purescript
mkTransformation identity (V3 1.0 2.0 3.0) -- pure translation
mkTransformation rotation (V3 0.0 0.0 0.0) -- pure rotation
```
declarations.title: mkTransformation

declarations.comments: Build a 4x4 transformation matrix from a 3x3 rotation matrix and translation.
declarations.title: mkTransformationMat

declarations.comments:
Multiply a matrix by a column vector (M * v).

```purescript
mulMV identity33 v = v
```
declarations.title: mulMV

declarations.comments: Multiply a row vector by a matrix (v * M).
declarations.title: mulVM

declarations.comments:
Matrix multiplication.

```purescript
mulMM identity33 m = m
mulMM m identity33 = m
```
declarations.title: mulMM

declarations.comments: Transpose a 2x2 matrix.
declarations.title: transpose22

declarations.comments: Transpose a 3x3 matrix.
declarations.title: transpose33

declarations.comments: Transpose a 4x4 matrix.
declarations.title: transpose44

declarations.comments: Determinant of a 2x2 matrix.
declarations.title: det22

declarations.comments: Determinant of a 3x3 matrix.
declarations.title: det33

declarations.comments: Determinant of a 4x4 matrix.
declarations.title: det44

declarations.comments: Inverse of a 2x2 matrix, if it exists.
declarations.title: inv22

declarations.comments: Inverse of a 3x3 matrix, if it exists.
declarations.title: inv33

declarations.comments: Inverse of a 4x4 matrix, if it exists.
declarations.title: inv44

declarations.title: diagonal33

declarations.comments: Compute the trace (sum of diagonal) of a 3x3 matrix.

modules.name: Linear.Matrix

modules.comments:
The Metric typeclass for inner products and norms.

This module provides the `Metric` typeclass which extends `Additive`
with operations for computing dot products, norms, and distances.

children.comments:
The inner (dot) product of two vectors.

```purescript
dot (V2 1.0 2.0) (V2 3.0 4.0) = 11.0  -- 1*3 + 2*4
```
children.title: dot

children.comments:
The squared norm of a vector (quadrance from rational trigonometry).

This is more efficient than `norm` when you only need to compare magnitudes.

```purescript
quadrance (V2 3.0 4.0) = 25.0  -- 3² + 4²
```
children.title: quadrance

children.comments:
The squared distance between two vectors.

```purescript
qd (V2 0.0 0.0) (V2 3.0 4.0) = 25.0
```
children.title: qd

children.comments:
The Euclidean distance between two vectors.

```purescript
distance (V2 0.0 0.0) (V2 3.0 4.0) = 5.0
```
children.title: distance

children.comments:
The Euclidean norm (magnitude) of a vector.

```purescript
norm (V2 3.0 4.0) = 5.0
```
children.title: norm

children.comments:
Convert a non-zero vector to a unit vector (signorm = "sign of norm").

Returns the zero vector when given the zero vector.

```purescript
signorm (V2 3.0 4.0) = V2 0.6 0.8
signorm (V2 0.0 0.0) = V2 0.0 0.0
```
children.title: signorm

declarations.comments:
A metric space with an inner product.

Laws:
- `dot v v >= 0` (positive semi-definite)
- `dot v v = 0` implies `v = zero`
- `dot u v = dot v u` (symmetry)
- `dot (a *^ u) v = a * dot u v` (linearity)
declarations.title: Metric

declarations.comments:
Normalize a vector to unit length.

Unlike `signorm`, this function handles the zero vector by returning
zero rather than NaN.

```purescript
normalize (V2 3.0 4.0) = V2 0.6 0.8
normalize (V2 0.0 0.0) = V2 0.0 0.0
```
declarations.title: normalize

declarations.comments:
Project the first vector onto the second.

```purescript
project (V2 1.0 0.0) (V2 1.0 1.0) = V2 0.5 0.5
```
declarations.title: project

modules.name: Linear.Metric

modules.comments:
Quaternions for 3D rotations.

This module provides quaternions, which are the most efficient and
numerically stable way to represent 3D rotations. Quaternions avoid
gimbal lock and interpolate smoothly.

declarations.comments:
A quaternion representing a rotation in 3D space.

A quaternion q = w + xi + yj + zk is stored as `Quaternion w (V3 x y z)`.
Unit quaternions (where |q| = 1) represent rotations.

```purescript
identity = Quaternion 1.0 (V3 0.0 0.0 0.0)
```
declarations.title: Quaternion

declarations.comments:
Construct a rotation quaternion from an axis and angle.

The axis should be a unit vector. The angle is in radians.

```purescript
-- 90 degree rotation around the Y axis
axisAngle (V3 0.0 1.0 0.0) (pi / 2.0)
```
declarations.title: axisAngle

declarations.comments:
Construct a rotation from Euler angles (in radians).

Order is ZYX (yaw, pitch, roll).

```purescript
fromEuler 0.0 0.0 0.0 = identity
```
declarations.title: fromEuler

declarations.comments:
Rotate a 3D vector by a quaternion.

The quaternion should be a unit quaternion for proper rotation.

```purescript
let q = axisAngle (V3 0.0 1.0 0.0) (pi / 2.0)  -- 90° around Y
rotate q (V3 1.0 0.0 0.0) ≈ V3 0.0 0.0 (-1.0)
```
declarations.title: rotate

declarations.comments:
The conjugate of a quaternion.

For unit quaternions, the conjugate equals the inverse and
represents the opposite rotation.

```purescript
conjugate (Quaternion w (V3 x y z)) = Quaternion w (V3 (-x) (-y) (-z))
```
declarations.title: conjugate

declarations.comments:
The multiplicative inverse of a quaternion.

For unit quaternions, this equals the conjugate.

```purescript
qmul q (inverse q) ≈ identity
```
declarations.title: inverse

declarations.comments:
Spherical linear interpolation between two quaternions.

This produces smooth rotation interpolation along the shortest path.

```purescript
slerp 0.0 q1 q2 = q1
slerp 1.0 q1 q2 = q2
slerp 0.5 q1 q2 -- halfway rotation
```
declarations.title: slerp

declarations.comments: Get the scalar (w) component.
declarations.title: getW

declarations.comments: Get the vector (xyz) components.
declarations.title: getXYZ

declarations.comments: Get the x component.
declarations.title: getX

declarations.comments: Get the y component.
declarations.title: getY

declarations.comments: Get the z component.
declarations.title: getZ

declarations.comments:
Quaternion multiplication (Hamilton product).

This is NOT commutative: `qmul q1 q2 /= qmul q2 q1` in general.
Composing rotations: to apply rotation q1 then q2, use `qmul q2 q1`.

```purescript
qmul identity q = q
qmul q identity = q
```
declarations.title: qmul

modules.name: Linear.Quaternion

modules.comments:
2-dimensional vectors.

This module provides the `V2` type for 2D vector operations,
commonly used for 2D graphics, physics, and game development.

declarations.comments:
A 2-dimensional vector.

```purescript
origin = V2 0.0 0.0
direction = V2 1.0 0.0
```
declarations.title: V2

declarations.comments:
The counter-clockwise perpendicular vector.

```purescript
perp (V2 1.0 0.0) = V2 0.0 1.0
perp (V2 0.0 1.0) = V2 (-1.0) 0.0
```
declarations.title: perp

declarations.comments:
Construct a unit vector at the given angle (in radians) from the positive x-axis.

```purescript
angle 0.0 = V2 1.0 0.0
angle (pi / 2.0) ≈ V2 0.0 1.0
```
declarations.title: angle

declarations.comments:
Extract the angle (in radians) from a vector, measured from the positive x-axis.

```purescript
unangle (V2 1.0 0.0) = 0.0
unangle (V2 0.0 1.0) = pi / 2.0
```
declarations.title: unangle

declarations.comments:
The z-component of the cross product of two 2D vectors.

This is useful for determining the orientation of two vectors:
- Positive: v2 is counter-clockwise from v1
- Negative: v2 is clockwise from v1
- Zero: vectors are parallel

```purescript
crossZ (V2 1.0 0.0) (V2 0.0 1.0) = 1.0  -- counter-clockwise
crossZ (V2 0.0 1.0) (V2 1.0 0.0) = -1.0 -- clockwise
```
declarations.title: crossZ

declarations.comments: Unit vector along the x-axis.
declarations.title: ex

declarations.comments: Unit vector along the y-axis.
declarations.title: ey

declarations.comments: Get the x component of a V2.
declarations.title: getX

declarations.comments: Get the y component of a V2.
declarations.title: getY

modules.name: Linear.V2

modules.comments:
3-dimensional vectors.

This module provides the `V3` type for 3D vector operations,
including the cross product for computing perpendicular vectors.

declarations.comments:
A 3-dimensional vector.

```purescript
origin = V3 0.0 0.0 0.0
up = V3 0.0 1.0 0.0
```
declarations.title: V3

declarations.comments:
The cross product of two 3D vectors.

The result is perpendicular to both input vectors, with magnitude
equal to the area of the parallelogram they span.

```purescript
cross (V3 1.0 0.0 0.0) (V3 0.0 1.0 0.0) = V3 0.0 0.0 1.0
```
declarations.title: cross

declarations.comments:
The scalar triple product of three vectors.

This equals the signed volume of the parallelepiped formed by the vectors.

```purescript
triple (V3 1.0 0.0 0.0) (V3 0.0 1.0 0.0) (V3 0.0 0.0 1.0) = 1.0
```
declarations.title: triple

declarations.comments: Unit vector along the x-axis.
declarations.title: ex

declarations.comments: Unit vector along the y-axis.
declarations.title: ey

declarations.comments: Unit vector along the z-axis.
declarations.title: ez

declarations.comments: Get the x component of a V3.
declarations.title: getX

declarations.comments: Get the y component of a V3.
declarations.title: getY

declarations.comments: Get the z component of a V3.
declarations.title: getZ

modules.name: Linear.V3

modules.comments:
4-dimensional vectors.

This module provides the `V4` type for 4D vector operations,
commonly used for homogeneous coordinates in 3D graphics.

declarations.comments:
A 4-dimensional vector.

Often used for homogeneous coordinates where (x, y, z, w) represents
the 3D point (x/w, y/w, z/w).

```purescript
origin = V4 0.0 0.0 0.0 1.0
direction = V4 1.0 0.0 0.0 0.0
```
declarations.title: V4

declarations.comments:
Convert a 3D vector to homogeneous coordinates with w=0.

This represents a direction (not a point) in homogeneous coordinates.

```purescript
vector (V3 1.0 0.0 0.0) = V4 1.0 0.0 0.0 0.0
```
declarations.title: vector

declarations.comments:
Convert a 3D point to homogeneous coordinates with w=1.

This represents a point (not a direction) in homogeneous coordinates.

```purescript
point (V3 1.0 2.0 3.0) = V4 1.0 2.0 3.0 1.0
```
declarations.title: point

declarations.comments:
Convert homogeneous coordinates back to 3D by dividing by w.

```purescript
normalizePoint (V4 2.0 4.0 6.0 2.0) = V3 1.0 2.0 3.0
```
declarations.title: normalizePoint

declarations.comments: Unit vector along the x-axis.
declarations.title: ex

declarations.comments: Unit vector along the y-axis.
declarations.title: ey

declarations.comments: Unit vector along the z-axis.
declarations.title: ez

declarations.comments: Unit vector along the w-axis.
declarations.title: ew

declarations.comments: Get the x component of a V4.
declarations.title: getX

declarations.comments: Get the y component of a V4.
declarations.title: getY

declarations.comments: Get the z component of a V4.
declarations.title: getZ

declarations.comments: Get the w component of a V4.
declarations.title: getW

modules.name: Linear.V4

modules.comments:
The Additive typeclass for vector spaces.

This module provides the foundational `Additive` typeclass that represents
vectors as an additive group, along with scalar multiplication operations.

children.comments: The zero vector
children.title: zero

children.comments: Vector addition
children.title: add

children.comments: Vector subtraction
children.title: sub

children.comments:
Linear interpolation: `lerp t a b = a + t * (b - a)`

- `lerp 0.0 a b = a`
- `lerp 1.0 a b = b`
children.title: lerp

children.comments: Apply a function to non-zero values (union semantics)
children.title: liftU2

children.comments: Apply a function component-wise (intersection semantics)
children.title: liftI2

declarations.comments:
A vector space with addition and scalar multiplication.

Laws:
- `add zero v = v` (left identity)
- `add v zero = v` (right identity)
- `add (add u v) w = add u (add v w)` (associativity)
- `add u v = add v u` (commutativity)
- `sub v v = zero`
declarations.title: Additive

declarations.comments:
Right scalar multiplication

```purescript
V2 1.0 2.0 ^* 3.0 = V2 3.0 6.0
```
declarations.title: scalarR

declarations.comments:
Left scalar multiplication

```purescript
3.0 *^ V2 1.0 2.0 = V2 3.0 6.0
```
declarations.title: scalarL

declarations.comments:
Right scalar division

```purescript
V2 6.0 9.0 ^/ 3.0 = V2 2.0 3.0
```
declarations.title: scalarDiv

declarations.comments:
Negate a vector

```purescript
negated (V2 1.0 (-2.0)) = V2 (-1.0) 2.0
```
declarations.title: negated

declarations.comments: Sum a collection of vectors
declarations.title: sumV

modules.name: Linear.Vector

packageMeta.description: Linear algebra for PureScript: vectors, matrices, quaternions (port of Edward Kmett's Haskell linear library)
packageMeta.name: purescript-linear
